WhenWillBTCRebound
13 mins ago
U.S. Justice Department Investigates Netflix for Potential Antitrust Violations
The U.S. Department of Justice is investigating Netflix for potential antitrust violations, according to a report by The Wall Street Journal. According to Jin10, the inquiry focuses on whether Netflix has engaged in exclusive practices that could lead to monopolistic behavior. The investigation is part of a broader effort by U.S. authorities to scrutinize major technology companies for anticompetitive practices. Netflix, a leading player in the streaming industry, has not yet commented on the investigation. The outcome of this inquiry could have significant implications for the company's business practices and the streaming industry as a whole.
14 mins ago
Smarter Web Company CEO Firm on Bitcoin Holdings
Andrew Webley, CEO of Smarter Web Company, expressed a strong commitment to retaining the company's Bitcoin assets in a recent interview with Bloomberg. According to NS3.AI, Webley emphasized that he would prefer to sell his arm rather than part with the company's Bitcoin holdings. Smarter Web Company currently possesses around 2,674 BTC and maintains a stable financial position, eliminating the need to liquidate any of its Bitcoin assets. The company is noted as one of the leading corporate Bitcoin accumulators in the UK.
15 mins ago
Kalshi Partners with Sleeper to Integrate Prediction Market
Kalshi has announced a partnership with the sports app Sleeper to incorporate its prediction market directly into the platform. According to NS3.AI, this collaboration allows Sleeper users to access Kalshi's prediction markets seamlessly within the sports app environment. This integration represents a strategic move by Kalshi to expand its market presence among sports-oriented users.
21 mins ago
Strategy Faces Liquidity Pressure as Convertible Debt Remains Out-of-the-Money
Strategy's convertible debt, amounting to $8.2 billion, is currently out-of-the-money, indicating that bondholders favor retaining bonds rather than converting them into common stock. According to NS3.AI, this scenario compels the company to persist in making cash interest payments and principal repayments until the debt matures. Without an increase in Strategy's stock price (MSTR), the bonds will continue to pose a financial strain, heightening the pressure on the company's liquidity.
29 mins ago
Technology and Industrials Lead Q4 S&P 500 Earnings Growth
Technology is leading the earnings growth for the S&P 500 in the fourth quarter, according to FactSet. FactSet posted on X, highlighting that the industrial sector holds the second position in earnings growth. The analysis by FactSet VP Senior Earnings Analyst John Butters provides insights into the performance of these sectors. For a detailed analysis, listeners are encouraged to explore further discussions on platforms like Apple Podcasts and Spotify.
30 mins ago
XRP Surges 18% Amid Bitcoin's Brief Rise Above $70,000
XRP witnessed a significant price increase of 18%, emerging as the leading performer among major cryptocurrencies. According to NS3.AI, this swift recovery happened as Bitcoin temporarily exceeded the $70,000 threshold following a steep drop the day before. These price fluctuations took place during U.S. morning trading hours, just before the weekend.
36 mins ago
49 S&P Stocks Surge Over 10% This Week
Macro Charts posted on X, highlighting a significant stock-picking environment. 49 S&P stocks rose more than 10% this week, while 155 stocks increased by 5-10%. Strong stocks strengthened, while weaker ones declined.
39 mins ago
Market Trends Show Potential Upswing Despite Recent Selloff
Jeffrey Hirsch, CEO of Hirsch Holdings and publisher of the Almanac Trader, posted on X that despite a steep selloff in recent days, market patterns suggest a potential upward trend in the coming days. Hirsch noted that while the recent decline was sharper than expected, the chart indicates a possible rise. The focus now shifts to observing how the market closes today to confirm this trend.
56 mins ago
New Federal Reserve Chair's Impact on S&P 500 Performance
Jeffrey Hirsch, CEO of Hirsch Holdings and publisher of the Almanac Trader, posted on X that the appointment of a new Federal Reserve Chair is not expected to have a bearish impact on the market. Historically, the S&P 500 has shown positive performance three months after such appointments, with a 60% likelihood of being higher. When excluding periods like Meyer’s Depression and Greenspan’s 1987 crash, the frequency and magnitude of gains increase. On average, the S&P 500 sees a 12.7% rise over one year, with a 90% chance of being higher.
57 mins ago
January ETF Analysis Reveals Trends in Asset Class and Sector
FactSet posted on X, revealing the latest analysis of Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs) for January. The report highlights trends in Assets Under Management (AUM) and fund flows across various asset classes and sectors. The analysis provides insights into investor behavior and market dynamics, offering a detailed look at how different sectors are performing in the current economic climate. This information is crucial for investors seeking to understand the shifts in investment strategies and market preferences. The report serves as a valuable resource for those looking to make informed decisions in the ETF market.
58 mins ago
S&P 500 Companies' Q4 EPS Performance Matches 10-Year Average
In the fourth quarter, 76% of companies in the S&P 500 index surpassed their earnings per share (EPS) estimates. FactSet posted on X that this figure is slightly below the five-year average of 78% but aligns with the 10-year average of 76%. The data highlights a consistent performance over the decade, despite a slight dip compared to more recent years. The earnings insight provides a perspective on the financial health and forecasting accuracy of these major companies.
1 hours ago
U.S. LNG Production Sees Increase in November
The U.S. Energy Information Administration reports a rise in U.S. liquefied natural gas (LNG) production for November. According to Jin10, production increased by 51,000 barrels per day, reaching a total of 7.849 million barrels per day, compared to 7.798 million barrels per day in October. This growth reflects ongoing developments in the energy sector.
1 hours ago
SEC Chair Atkins Highlights US Crypto Leadership Under Trump
Scott Melker, host of The Wolf Of All Streets Podcast, posted on X that SEC Chair Atkins emphasized the impact of Trump's leadership. He noted a new era of coordination between the CFTC and SEC. Atkins stated that these efforts are positioning the U.S. as the crypto capital of the world, showcasing the country's commitment to advancing its crypto industry.
1 hours ago
U.S. Treasury Secretary Advocates Strong Dollar Policy
U.S. Treasury Secretary Besant has emphasized the importance of implementing a strong dollar policy. According to ChainCatcher, this approach necessitates measures to create a robust support environment for the U.S. dollar.
1 hours ago
Scott Melker Declares Bull Market Return
Scott Melker, host of The Wolf Of All Streets Podcast, posted on X, announcing the return of the bull market. He tagged several financial experts in his post.
1 hours ago
Fidelity Launches Stablecoin Pegged to U.S. Dollar
Fidelity Investments has introduced the Fidelity Digital Dollar (FIDD), a stablecoin pegged 1:1 to the U.S. dollar, in compliance with the GENIUS Act. According to NS3.AI, the stablecoin is accessible to both retail and institutional clients through Fidelity's platforms and third-party exchanges where it is listed. This initiative signifies Fidelity's entry into the stablecoin market, utilizing the Ethereum blockchain.
1 hours ago
Chubb Exceeds Expectations with Strong Fourth Quarter Performance
Chubb has reported its fourth-quarter earnings per share (EPS), surpassing expectations with a figure of $7.52 compared to FactSet's forecast of $6.77. FactSet posted on X, highlighting Chubb's record investment performance and robust underwriting results as key contributors to this achievement. The report also identifies two macroeconomic factors that are favorable for the company. Additionally, it provides insights into the processes involved in basic underwriting and investment income data analysis.
1 hours ago
Crypto Futures Liquidations Reach $2.04 Billion Amid Market Volatility
Crypto futures markets have seen significant liquidations, with $145 million wiped out in just one hour. According to NS3.AI, over the past 24 hours, approximately $2.04 billion in futures contracts were liquidated. These figures underscore the heightened market volatility and intense selling pressure on leveraged positions. The rapid liquidation of futures contracts reflects the current instability in the cryptocurrency market, as traders face challenges in managing leveraged positions amid fluctuating prices. The substantial liquidations indicate a cautious sentiment among investors, who are reassessing their strategies in response to the volatile market conditions.
1 hours ago
Federal Reserve Vice Chair Jefferson Affirms Credibility on Inflation Target
Federal Reserve Vice Chair Jefferson has stated that anchored expectations indicate the Federal Reserve maintains credibility regarding its inflation target. According to ChainCatcher, this assertion underscores the central bank's commitment to managing inflation effectively.
1 hours ago
Cardano Rebounds 17% Amid Weak Market Sentiment
Cardano's price has experienced a 17% rebound following a sharp 20% decline. According to NS3.AI, this recovery was supported by strong whale accumulation and significant exchange outflows during the downturn. Despite this, market sentiment remains weak, with a 90% drop in positive sentiment metrics, posing a risk to sustained recovery. Key technical levels between $0.22 and $0.26 will be crucial in determining if the rebound can hold, with a potential upside toward $0.30 if sentiment improves.

Binance ADGM entities are regulated by the Financial Services Regulatory Authority (FSRA) of the Abu Dhabi Global Markets (ADGM) as follows: (1) Nest Exchange Limited is recognised as a Recognised Investment Exchange (Derivatives), with a stipulation to Operate a Multilateral Trading Facility; (2) Nest Clearing and Custody Limited is recognised as a Recognised Clearing House, with a stipulation to Provide Custody and operating a Central Securities Depository; (3) Nest Trading Limited is authorised to carry out the following Regulated Activities: (i) Dealing in Investments as Principal; (ii) Dealing in Investments as Agent; (iii) Arranging Deals in Investments; (iv) Managing Assets; (v) Providing Money Services; and (vi) Arranging Custody.

Risk Warning: virtual asset prices can be volatile. The value of your investment may go down or up and you may not get back the amount invested. You are solely responsible for your investment decisions and Binance is not liable for any trading losses you may incur.
Binance© 2026Cookie Preferences

Privacy Preference Center

When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. However, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.
Cookie Policy

Manage Consent Preferences

Strictly Necessary Cookies

Always Active

These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.

Functional Cookies

These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.

Targeting Cookies

Targeting cookies may be set through our site by ourselves and our advertising partners. First parties and third parties will use them to build a profile of your interests based on the browsing information they collect from you, which includes uniquely identifying your browser and terminal equipment. If you do not allow these cookies you will still see basic advertising on your browser that is generic and not based on your interests

Performance Cookies

These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. All information these cookies collect is aggregated and therefore anonymous. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.
